question_text: A shoe on concrete provides good grip, but the same shoe on wet ice has very poor grip. What is the main reason for this difference?
correct_answer: Concrete is rough and has a high coefficient of friction with rubber, while ice is smooth and slippery with a low coefficient of friction, plus the water layer reduces contact between the shoe and ice surface.
explanation: Different material pairs have different levels of friction. Rough concrete grips well, but ice is very smooth and slippery, especially when wet, so friction is much lower.
